The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification: both check that res equals the input list l with each element incremented by 1. The Coq uses List.map with (fun x => x + 1) and the Python uses a list comprehension [x + 1 for x in l], which produce the same concrete results for any list of integers.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. Both check that res equals the list obtained by adding 1 to each element of l. The Coq uses List.map with (fun x => x + 1) and the Python uses a list comprehension [x + 1 for x in l], which produce the same concrete results for any list of integers.

Both compute res equals map (+1) over the input list; Python ints match Coq Z behavior, so results align on all valid inputs.
Both check that res equals l with each element incremented by 1; Python ints match Coq Z and list equality matches structural equality.
Both define res as the elementwise increment of l by 1; Python ints and lists match Coq Z and list behavior for all concrete integer lists.
